mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with stable inflation indicates robust corporate activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day returns in triggered instances are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment and declining unemployment signals strong labor market dynamics that boost consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both PAYEMS and UNRATE move in expected directions.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation pressures, as measured by the CPI, signal accommodative monetary policy that supports equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods with CPIAUCSL below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending, as indicated by retail sales, signals strong demand conditions that drive corporate revenues and equity prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth combined with rising housing starts indicates robust demand conditions that support equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns in triggered instances are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with low interest rates indicates favorable supply-side conditions and monetary support, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both INDPRO and FEDFUNDS meet trigger conditions.
